commonIssues:
Users report server errors, "failed to submit" messages, and high traffic during peak hours, often resulting in failed or slow submissions.

How to fix:

  • Try accessing the Banglarbhumi portal during early morning or late night hours for better server response. Banglarbhumi

A major hurdle is entering incorrect Khatian or Plot (Dag) numbers, leading to applications being rejected or showing "no record found".

How to fix:

  • Double-check Khatian and Plot (Dag) numbers from physical RoR documents before entering them online.

Inability to scan documents properly or failure to adhere to specific file formats and size limits often causes uploads to be rejected.

How to fix:

  • Use online tools to compress PDFs to exactly under 2MB and ensure they are clear before uploading.

Difficulty in selecting the correct Mouza or understanding the difference between RS and LR records, causing wrong information to be submitted.

How to fix:

  • Consult a local deed writer (Muharrir) or BLRO office to clarify RS and LR record differences.

Inaccurately calculating stamp duty or registration fees in the e-requisition form, leading to payment errors.

How to fix:

  • Use the official e-Nathikaran portal's stamp duty calculator. e-Nathikaran

Mutation is often delayed for months, with the portal showing "under process", requiring follow-up visits to the BLRO office.

How to fix:

  • Track your mutation status online on Banglarbhumi and visit the Block Land and Land Reforms Officer (BLRO) if stuck.

Mutation applications are sometimes rejected without a specific explanation, forcing users to start the process over.

How to fix:

  • File an RTI or visit the BLRO office to understand the exact deficiency in your application.

If the Pit Dalil (Chain Deed) is incomplete, meaning previous ownership transfers aren't documented, the current mutation is rejected.

How to fix:

  • Obtain certified copies of the missing link deeds (Pit Dalil) from the registry office.

Information on the Banglarbhumi portal sometimes conflicts with physical documents, a legacy of inaccurate digitization.

How to fix:

  • Apply for data correction under Section 50 of the WBLR Act at the BLRO office.

Failure to attend physical hearings scheduled by the BLRO can lead to application cancellation.

How to fix:

  • Regularly check the hearing notice section on Banglarbhumi and attend scheduled hearings with original documents.

Delays in obtaining conversions from agricultural to residential/commercial use if the plot is in a restricted area.

How to fix:

  • Apply for Section 4C conversion online on Banglarbhumi and follow up with the SDLRO.
